title: Wire Google Custom Search engine ID into backend search integration

requirements.text: Update the backend Google Custom Search integration to use the search engine ID 'a566dfe30b89f4713' as the `cx` parameter in all outbound Google Custom Search API requests.

quotes: make this website powered by google search here is my google search id a566dfe30b89f4713

acceptanceCriteria: The `cx` query parameter in the Google Custom Search API URL is set to 'a566dfe30b89f4713'.
Search queries submitted through the browser return real Google search results from the specified custom search engine.
No hardcoded placeholder or empty `cx` value remains in the backend search handler.

requirements.text: Ensure the frontend search flow correctly passes search queries to the backend, receives Google Custom Search results, and displays them in the SearchResults component without errors.

quotes: make this website powered by google search

acceptanceCriteria: Typing a search query in the address bar and pressing Enter triggers a search request to the backend.
Search results are rendered in the SearchResults component with title, snippet, and display URL.
Clicking a search result navigates the active tab to the result URL via the proxy.
No console errors appear during a normal search flow.

constraints: Do not change the frontend immutable paths (useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui).
Keep all backend logic inside the single main actor in backend/main.mo.
The search engine ID must be exactly 'a566dfe30b89f4713' — do not alter it.
nonGoals: Adding any new UI features beyond wiring the existing search engine ID.
Supporting multiple search engine IDs or user-configurable search engines.
